chunks.text: 2017-18 Archive of Special Events - EECS at Berkeley 2017-18 Archive of Special Events WiCSE 40th Anniversary The UC Berkeley Women in Computer Science and Engineering (WiCSE) 40th Anniversary Reunion, which celebrates 40 years of women in technology at Berkeley, will be held on Saturday, March 17, 2018. Join us for a day packed full of reunions, enlightening talks, and networking opportunities! Learn More About WICSE's 40th Get Ready for Big Give! Big Give, UC Berkeley’s annual fundraising outreach to alumni, students, parents, faculty, staff, and friends will happen between 9 pm on Wednesday, March 13 and 9 pm on Thursday, March 14! With your help, we can remain one of the finest departments in the No. 1 public university in the world. We hope you will join us! Support EECS for Big Give BEARS 2018 After nearly 50 years of Moore’s Law, and many premature predictions of its demise along the way, it is now clear that “conventional” transistor scaling is finally reaching fundamental limits. This year’s BEARS conference will highlight some of the latest thinking and results that show a bright future for electronics and computing. Learn More About BEARS 2018 Symposium Tribute to Lotfi Zadeh The Symposium of Fuzzy Logic and Fuzzy Sets on February 5, 2018, honors of the life and research of pioneer Lotfi Zadeh, widely recognized as the “Father of Fuzzy Logic.” Speakers will discuss Zadeh’s wide-ranging influence and lasting contributions to computer science, statistical analysis, and the foundations of mathematics.
